message:
refactor: clippy fixes, API naming improvements, and sharding property test hardening

Code quality improvements across multiple crates addressing clippy warnings
and improving API clarity:

Clippy fixes:
- cpu_pressure.rs: suppress dead-code warning for ncpu field on non-Linux
  platforms by referencing it in the cfg(linux) branch
- orphan_reaper.rs: add #[cfg(any(target_os = "macos", test))] to
  parse_etime() so it is only compiled on macOS or in test builds,
  eliminating dead-code warning on Linux
- config/daemon.rs: remove unnecessary `as u32` cast on libc::S_ISVTX
  which is already the correct type
- procinfo/linux.rs: flatten nested if-let chain using Iterator::flatten()
  to reduce indentation depth (clippy::manual_flatten)

API improvements:
- layout.rs: add PaneStack::is_empty() (clippy::len_without_is_empty);
  rename LayoutCycle::next() to advance() to avoid shadowing the Iterator
  trait method; use indexed iteration in redistribute_panes instead of
  manual indexing
- tab.rs: update call sites for advance() rename; use HashMap::values()
  iterator instead of destructuring unused key in all_stacked_pane_ids()
- process_tree.rs: rename local variable ppid to parent_pid for clarity

Sharding property test hardening (proptest_sharding.rs, +100 lines):
- Add arb_assignment_strategy_for_shard_count() generator that produces
  valid strategies (RoundRobin, ConsistentHash, ByDomain, ByAgentType,
  Manual) parameterized by shard count
- Add VendoredShardingConfig serde roundtrip + validation property test
  to catch serialization drift
- Increase consistent hash minimal-disruption test range from 20..400 to
  120..800 pane IDs and tighten the assertion from "remapped < total" to
  "remapped <= ceil(total/N)" where N is original shard count, matching
  the theoretical ~1/N remapping guarantee of consistent hashing

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -318,7 +318,7 @@ fn read_process_info(pid: u32) -> Option<RawProcessInfo> {
         .first()
         .map(|s| parse_linux_state(s))
         .unwrap_or(ProcessState::Unknown);
-    let ppid = fields
+    let parent_pid = fields
         .get(1)
         .and_then(|s| s.parse::<u32>().ok())
         .unwrap_or(0);
@@ -344,7 +344,7 @@ fn read_process_info(pid: u32) -> Option<RawProcessInfo> {
 
     Some(RawProcessInfo {
         pid,
-        ppid,
+        ppid: parent_pid,
         name,
         argv,
         state,
